_ref: BS EN 62305-2:2012 — number of dangerous events (Annex A), probabilities of damage (Annex B), and relative losses (Annex C). Values are the standard's tabulated typical/default figures; a project-specific assessment may differ. Defaults chosen here are conservative for screening. Distances: A_M uses the 500 m near-strike radius; lines use the simplified A_L = 40·L_L and A_I = 4000·L_L (Table A.3).

near._ref: Annex A — near-strike collection radius for induced surges (R_M).

line._ref: Table A.3 simplified collection areas (m², L_L in m).

locationCd._ref: Table A.1 — C_D relative location of the structure.

lineCi._ref: Table A.4 — C_I line installation factor.

lineCe._ref: Table A.5 — C_E line environmental factor.

lineCt._ref: Table A.3 — C_T HV/LV transformer factor on the line.

pB_byClass._ref: Table B.2 — P_B, physical damage to the structure vs. LPS class.

pSPD_byLevel._ref: Table B.3 — P_SPD, failure of internal systems vs. coordinated SPD protection level (SPL).

pEB_byLevel._ref: Table B.7 — P_EB, equipotential bonding vs. SPL of bonding SPDs.

pLD_shieldFactor._ref: Table B.8 — shielding/bonding column of P_LD (flash TO a line). Multiplied by the U_w withstand reduction below to give P_LD.

pLI_shieldFactor._ref: Table B.9 — shielding/bonding column of P_LI (flash NEAR a line). Multiplied by the U_w withstand reduction below.

uwReduction._ref: Table B.8/B.9 — reduction of P_LD/P_LI with the rated impulse withstand voltage U_w (kV) of the equipment. Normalised to 1.0 at U_w = 1 kV (unshielded line, U_w = 2.5 kV → 0.3 reproduces Table B.8). For an intermediate U_w the next lower band is used (conservative).

pTA._ref: Table B.1 — P_TA, additional protection against touch/step voltage (flash to structure).

pTU._ref: Table B.6 — P_TU, protection against touch voltage (flash to line).

ks._ref: Annex B.5 — P_MS = (K_S1·K_S2·K_S3·K_S4)². K_S1 = 0.12·w_m1 (large-scale spatial shield mesh width, m), K_S2 = 0.12·w_m2 (inner-LPZ shield mesh width, m), K_S4 = 1/U_w (U_w in kV). K_S3 from the table below. Each capped at 1.0; no shield ⇒ factor 1.0.

ks3._ref: Table B.5 — K_S3 by internal-wiring routing / shielding.

loss._ref: Annex C — typical relative losses and reduction factors (BS EN 62305-2:2012).

LT._ref: Table C.2 — L_T, injury by touch/step voltage. L1 (life) = 1e-2; L4 (economic, livestock) = 1e-4.

LF._ref: Table C.2 — L_F, physical damage. L1/L2/L3 ≈ 1e-1 typical (refined by r_f, r_p, h_z); L4 = 0.5.

LO._ref: Table C.2 — L_O, failure of internal systems. L1 only where failure endangers life (hospital ICU / explosion) = 1e-1, else 0; L2 = 1e-2; L4 = 1e-2.

rt._ref: Table C.3 — r_t, reduction by ground/floor surface type (touch/step).

rp._ref: Table C.4 — r_p, reduction by fire-protection provisions.

rf._ref: Table C.5 — r_f, reduction by risk of fire/explosion.

hz._ref: Table C.6 — h_z, increase factor for special hazard (L1 only).
